case_name: NIST Hacking Case (Greg Schardt / Mr. Evil)
description: On 09/20/04, a Dell CPi laptop serial VLQLW was found abandoned with a wireless PCMCIA card and a homemade 802.11b antenna. The suspect Greg Schardt alias Mr. Evil was war driving near public WiFi hotspots to intercept traffic and steal credentials.
tier_note: Ground truth verificable. Usar para scoring de precisión.

artifacts.content: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\RegisteredOwner = Greg Schardt | HKLM\SYSTEM\ControlSet001\Control\ComputerName\ComputerName = N-1A9ODN6ZXK4LQ | HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\DefaultUserName = Mr. Evil
forensic_anomalies: The default username is Mr. Evil, indicative of an attacker alias
The registered owner matches the identified suspect

artifacts.content: Intercepted pcap file at C:\Documents and Settings\Mr. Evil\interception | Victim: Windows CE Pocket PC | Sites accessed: login.passport.com, mobile.msn.com, MSN Hotmail | Capture performed with Ethereal
forensic_anomalies: pcap file with intercepted traffic from third-party devices
The victim was using a Windows CE Pocket PC at a public hotspot

artifacts.content: IRC channels accessed: #Chataholics.UnderNet, #CyberCafe.UnderNet, #Elite.Hackers.UnderNet, #evilfork.EFnet, #ISO-WAREZ.EFnet, #thedarktower.AfterNET | IRC user: Mini Me, Nick: Mr, Alt: mrevilrulez
forensic_anomalies: The nickname mrevilrulez matches the email mrevilrulez@yahoo.com

artifacts.content: Files in Recycle Bin: Dc1.exe (lalsetup250.exe - Look@LAN installer), Dc2.exe (netstumblerinstaller_0_4_0.exe), Dc3.exe (WinPcap_3_01_a.exe), Dc4.exe (ethereal-setup-0.10.6.exe) | Image MD5 hash: aee4fcd9301c03b3b054623ca261959a
forensic_anomalies: Presence of hacking tool executables in Recycle Bin without permanent deletion
NetStumbler is a specific tool for war driving

artifacts.content: SMTP: whoknowsme@sbcglobal.net | Primary email: mrevilrulez@yahoo.com | NNTP: Configured for 5+ newsgroups | Programs: Outlook Express, mIRC
forensic_anomalies: SMTP address different from primary email (possible evasion)
Use of newsgroups for alternative communication
